studies.duration_additional_info: The Caterna visual training games are played for 90 days (13 weeks) daily for 30-45 minutes during occlusion. The prospective data analysis will take place over a period of 5 years.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention involves the use of Caterna online visual training, which is used in addition to occlusion therapy. The Caterna visual training games are played on the computer for 90 days daily for 30-45 minutes during occlusion.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: Eye patching therapy will be continued until the worse eye reaches the same visual acuity as the better eye or there is no further improvement of the visual acuity of the worse eye. Check-ups are regular check-ups by orthoptists and ophthalmologists. The Caterna visual training is designed to keep patients alert and is played on the computer at home for 30-45 minutes/day during the patching period for at least 90 days. Secondary Outcome: Optimization of amblyopia therapy through data analysis of a Germany-wide network.

studies.duration_additional_info: The study analyzed children with at least 90 days of prior occlusion therapy before intervention.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received focal ambient visual acuity stimulation (FAVAS) alongside occlusion therapy, while the control group received occlusion therapy alone.
studies.collected_data: Best corrected visual acuity (BCVA, logMAR) as the primary outcome; stereo vision as the secondary outcome.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: The FAVAS group showed a significant improvement in BCVA, with a mean gain of 0.15 logMAR (95% CI [0.11;0.18], p < 0.001), compared to a non-significant improvement of 0.01 logMAR (95% CI [-0.02;0.04], p = 0.356) in the control group. No significant changes were observed in stereo vision in either group.

audit.study_title: Multi-center retrospective and prospective data analysis with additive app-based therapy to optimize amblyopia therapy
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Focal ambient visual acuity stimulation improves vision in amblyopic children with stagnating BCVA-A retrospective study.
